references.title: Learning Behavior-oriented Knowledge Tracing
references.journal: Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Answering question at Step t+1 correctly
extracted.Students: 3006 ASSISTments 2009 learners, 22397 ASSISTments 2012 learners, 1000 Juniy learners
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: Previous models tend to look at KT as sequences of answers, leaving behind the different types of learner behavior that are relevant to model a learner’s knowledge state.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
LBKT has 2 modules: 

Differentiated Behavior Effect Quantifying: it looks at 3 specific learning behaviors (Speed, Attempts and Hints). 

Fused Behavior Effect Measuring (FBEM): Behaviors are not independent from one another. For that reason, the authors use a separate module to measure dependencies between behavioral features.
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Learners are represented as matrix that is updated with more and more exercises. The update of knowledge states includes a forget gate and a knowledge acquisition vector.
extracted.Models: DKT, DKT_concat, AT-DKT, DFVMN, DDMKT, SAKT, AKT, LPKT, LBKT-DB, LBKT-FB, LBKT-Forget
extracted.Comments: Authors create a new methodd that looks to introduce Learner Behavior. Model is validated on 3 public datasets. Multiple Ablation Studies are also performed to measure sensitiviy of the model to different parameters.
extracted.Limitations:
No Cross-Validation,

Limited number of learner behaviors considered in approach.

annotations.text: Most of the KT work that exists is based on predicting whether a student will get a question right or wrong, often disregarding relevant indicators such as answering speed or number of attempts. Authors propose LBKT which is meant to explore these constructs.

Authors start with explanation of the constructs and proceed with their quantification. Benchmarking on public datasets shows that the model outperforms other approaches in the literature. Code is also publicly available.
